messages.message: I'm watching the Tyrolia and Iberian regions closely. If you're considering a push into Tyrolia, I'd be happy to support your army from Venice in exchange for a future favor.

Hi Germany! Would you be interested in cooperating to stop the western juggernaut that so frequently dominates the game? I think that our two nations make natural allies given our central positioning and how our forces can move outward from one another without being a threat to each other!
I see your interest in the Mediterranean. If you're looking to secure southern routes, I might be able to assist in keeping the region stable—perhaps we could discuss a mutual benefit.

Greetings!
I reckon a juggernaut could be quite effective and easy to disguise early on. Would you be interested in that?

Hey France!
Just reaching out to say hi and establish comms. I think we both benefit a lot from working together and that would require that we establish a DMZ in the channel. I think if you were willing to allow me into Bel in the fall I could build a fleet in EDI and help you out against Germany. What do you think?
Hi Austria! Italy is a common threat to both of us, so I think it would be beneficial if we worked together to contain the threat from Italy, as it often dominates in games.

Hey Russia!
Just reaching out to say hi. I think it would be beneficial for both of us if we stayed out of each other's hair for as long as possible, and that would require you to not move an army to Stp in the fall. What do you think?
Hi France, I definitely agree that Italy is a common threat. I'm going to focus on the east for a bit, but if you want to set up a mid game attack I could probably be convinced.

Hey Germany!
Just reaching out to say hi. I think we could really benefit from working together. I know that Germany is a tricky power to play, and there's not much that I can do to help you with that, but I think it's really important that we maintain friendly relations in the beginning. What are your thoughts?

I absolutely would! I'm a huge fan of them as Turkey as well, I think they give you a much more fluid play than a RT.
Have you talked to anyone else about this yet? How much of the other powers are you getting messages from?

I think a bounce in the black sea would be useful for both of us in 1901, then I can support myself into Rum in the fall and you can sneak into Greece!
I noticed your fleet in the Aegean. If you're considering expanding westward, I’d be interested in hearing how we might support one another’s ambitions.

I would very much like to secure the Mediterranean. The surrounding territories would be much easier for me to secure in an alliance with you than if I were to attack you first, and I am sure we would be able to reach an agreement as to the distribution of Scs.

I'd love to avoid allying Germany if at all possible!
If that was something you were considering, I'd be open to a bounce in Norway to hide the alliance

Hi there Germany!
Just wanted to check in with you and see how you're feeling about Sweden.
Are you planning on letting me into it in the fall, or would you prefer to arrange a bounce?
Sounds good to me. I'll be sure to keep you updated.

Hi France,
Wanted to reach out and say hi! Let me know if you have any thoughts on openings, I'm always happy to swap info
Sounds good. I'm not quite sure of my opening moves yet, but I will be sure to keep you updated!
Sure. That sounds good to me. A move to Stp from MOS is very anti English, and a move to Stp from STP would be bad for me, so I'm more than happy to bounce you in Norway.
Hi Italy, I'm definitely considering a push to Tyrolia, but I'm not sure I want to move there this turn. Let me get back to you.
For the moment, I have to wait how everybody behaves towards me, but if things will go well, I would be interested in supporting you in Greece.

Hi England! I'm glad to hear that you'd like to work together, though I would certainly like to establish a DMZ in the Channel. I will not contest your claim on Belgium, so long as I can count on your support in my future efforts against Germany. Would you also be interested in aligning ourselves against Germany? I think we'd have the same long-term goal in mind.
I agree. We both benefit from getting to midgame together, so I'd love to work together. If you have any ideas on how to start, feel free to let me know!
Great! sounds like a plan
Absolutely! What are your thoughts on opening moves? I'd like to see if we can find a way to get you into Bel this year, so that we can both get two builds.

Hey there Austria!
Wanted to check in and see how you're feeling towards working together this game!
Hey there Austria. I don't know about you, but I think it goes without saying that we should have a pretty good alliance in the early game. What are your thoughts?
Hey Italy, how are you doing? Just wanted to see if you've had any thoughts on the early game, and if there's anything I can do to help you out.
Hi Germany, I'm on board.

Hey Italy!
I always find that R/I is a great mid-late game alliance if the two of us do well. If you have any questions about your opening, or would like to collaborate on anything, let me know
Hey there Turkey. Just wanted to see if you've had any thoughts on the early game, and if there's anything I can do to help you out.
Excellent. Any idea on how we should open?
That would be much appreciated. I'm not sure of my opening moves yet, but I'll be sure to let you know!

Hey there Russia, how are you?
I don't know, I haven't heard much from England, so I don't know if I could say either way just yet. How's your relationship with Austria? I'd love to work with you two.
Sure. I'm considering opening with a classic 'All or nothing' opening, so I'd be up for attacking France together in 1902 or 1903. Does that sound agreeable to you?
Hi Russia, I'm not totally opposed, but I'd like to see how the first turn or two go first.

That sounds intriguing—I'll keep Greece in mind as a potential area of mutual interest. For now, I'm focusing on securing my own borders and ensuring stability in the Mediterranean. Let’s stay in touch about how we might align our goals.
Sounds good to me! Yes, I would like to help you against Germany. It would be hard for us to expand without attacking him at some point.
Hi Germany! I'm always interested in cooperation with central powers so I'll keep in touch.
Sounds good! I'll be opening to Den, Kiel, and Ruhr.
That sounds good. If you open to EC, I'll open to Bur.
Ok, thank you.
Well I've got a secure border with you, and Italy has seemed keen on containing France. I'm going to try to keep a solid alliance with Italy as long as possible, but we'll see.
Excellent!

I’m keeping my options open for the early game, but I’m especially watching the Tyrolia and Adriatic regions. If you're considering a move into Tyrolia, I'd be happy to support your efforts in exchange for a future favor.
I don't think I will be, but thanks for the offer! I'll keep you updated if that changes.
Perfect. My opening moves will be quite standard, so as to not arouse suspicion. My plan is to move my fleet in Brest into the Mid-atlantic Ocean, and to move my armies to Picardy and Burgundy.
Cool, that sounds good. Italy reached out to me about possibly moving to Tyr, but I told him I probably wouldn't.
Perfect. My plan is to move my fleet into Mid-atlantic Ocean, and to move my armies to Picardy and Burgundy.
Okay. I was thinking that if you move to Pic, then we could get you into Bel, rather than bouncing. Then I'd be able to guarantee that I'd get into Hol.

A R/I alliance could certainly be powerful in the mid-game. I’m currently focused on securing southern routes and monitoring Austria’s movements. If our interests align in the Balkans or Greece, I’d be happy to explore a collaboration.
Ok, let's do the bounce in the black sea and I'll try to move to Greece.
Sounds good! Have you talked to Turkey about the balkans yet?
Do you know anything about Austrian plans? I haven't received any messages from them.
I'm telling him the same thing. I'm moving to Galicia. If you move to Silesia, we can take Warsaw next year.
Same here, how are you feeling about Gal? bounce?
Sounds good to me!
I think we should keep the peace between us. You go west, and I go east, and we'll leave each other alone. Sound good?
Sounds good!

Hi Turkey! Our two nations often share interests in the Mediterranean, so I wanted to reach out and open communications. If you would like to work together in some way, I will be sure to keep you updated!
That sounds like a pretty good plan.
Awesome.

I will probably be doing some kind of traditional opening to get two Scs this year. I'm not too sure how much you'll be able to influence my movements at this point, but we may be able to work together in the future!

I haven’t spoken with Turkey about the Balkans yet—I’m waiting for clearer signals about their long-term plans. However, if you’re interested in a working understanding in the region, I’m certainly open to exploring it. Let me know where your focus lies.
I'd love to get a I/R alliance going. Have you talked to Turkey about it yet?
Sounds good, let's keep in touch
Hi France! Of course we share interests in the mediterranean. I'll be looking forward to work with you.

Austria has been quiet with me so far, but I believe they’re watching Tyrolia and the Adriatic closely. If you’re concerned about them moving toward the Aegean or Greece, I’d be interested in coordinating our strategies.
I'm moving there this turn, and probably bouncing. I wouldn't want to end up with you there.
Yes, I'm concerned about Austria. I'll probably move to Bulgaria and the Aegean in the first turn so we'll be able to cooperate if Austria decides to move south.
Hi Turkey, how are you feeling about an alliance?

Hey Italy!

Just reaching out to say hi. There's not much we can do to help each other this early in the game, but I'd be happy to share any useful information with you that I get. What do you think?

Sounds good.

I haven't heard much from Austria yet as well, other than a bounce in Galicia.
How do you feel about the west at the moment?
I've just received a message from Austria and he's asking for alliance. This may be a bluff, but I'll keep you updated.
Great!

Hey Austria!

Just reaching out to say hi. There's not much we can do to help each other this early in the game, but I'd be happy to share any useful information with you that I get. What do you think?
How are you feeling about Greece?
Your suggestion makes sense—I’d be happy to leave things peaceful between us for now. If you’re heading east, I’ll respect that space. Should your plans shift, though, I’d appreciate a heads-up.
I'd probably say I'm pretty good.
Hi Austria, I'll be open to that proposal. What exactly are your thoughts?

Hey Turkey!

Just reaching out to say hi. There's not much we can do to help each other this early in the game, but I'd be happy to share any useful information with you that I get. What do you think?
Great, glad to hear it!
What are your relations with Austria? They just contacted me to propose an alliance.

Sure. That sounds good to me. I'll be opening with a fairly standard opening as well. I'm going to be moving my fleets north to force Bel, and then moving to Norwegian Sea and North Sea with my armies.
I will, and I agree completely.
If I can get it, I'll take it.
What are your thoughts on Turkey?
France has told me he's moving to PIC and BUR. If you moved to BUR and I to EC then we'd probably be able to stop him from getting any builds.
Hi England, that sounds good. I'll do the same.

Hello England! While our direct paths may not cross much in the early game, I appreciate your offer to share information. If you notice anything about France or Germany’s movements that might affect southern Europe or the Mediterranean, I’d certainly appreciate the heads-up.
I'll take Serbia and Greece, you take Bulgaria and Rumania, and then we'll take on Russia.
Sounds good to me.
Cool. Best of luck!

I'm leaning towards an alliance, I think they're a strong midgame ally if we do well in the beginning. If we can find a way to work with you and Italy, I think it would help the three of us to try to make a long term central triple alliance.
I'm sure he'll go for Rumania, but if he doesn't he's going to find himself in a rough place pretty early on.
Great

An Austrian alliance offer could be a ploy or genuine — I’ll be watching closely how it unfolds. If Austria shifts toward the Aegean or Greece, I’ll be ready to act in ways that could complement your goals. Keep me posted on their stance.
Have you thought any more about the Balkans yet?
I'd definitely be interested in a triple. Have you talked to Italy?
I have. What do you think about them?
Sure thing! France has told me he'll be opening with a fairly standard opening (presumably that means BRE-MAO and MAR-SPA).
But I have no idea what the army in MAR will do.
Hi England, I think we can be of use to each other if you're planning to take on Russia early on.
That's ok with me, but I think it's too soon to attack Russia.
They seem okay, but not necessarily committed.
Probably, but the longer you wait the stronger he gets.

Your assurance is noted and appreciated. I’ll keep our eastern border quiet for now. If your plans evolve toward the Adriatic or further south, feel free to let me know — I’m always open to strategic clarity.
Yeah, that's what I've been thinking.
Good with me. What was he thinking?

I’m still evaluating the Balkans, but I’m watching Austria’s positioning closely. If they move aggressively toward the Adriatic or into Greece, I’d be open to coordinating with you to ensure a favorable outcome for both of us. Keep me updated on your own plans for the region.
He wants to move against Russia. It could be a bluff, I think he and Russia are allied.
I appreciate that. Good luck out west.

Thanks for the insight on France’s opening moves. If their army from MAR heads south toward the Mediterranean, I’d appreciate knowing—particularly if it impacts Italy’s southern borders or the Iberian approach.
Great! sounds good.
How are you feeling about Austria and Turkey?

Austria and Turkey remain cautious for now—I’m keeping a close eye on both. Austria seems focused on Tyrolia and the Adriatic, while Turkey is watching the Aegean and possibly Greece. If either shifts toward the Balkans, I’ll be ready to align with you there. For now, I’m focused on maintaining stability along my own borders.
Austria just told me he wants to move against Russia and Germany. But this could be a bluff too, I think he and Russia are allied.
I'll try to find out more.
Perfect!

I'm wary of a Turkish/austrian alliance at this point, and I think it would benefit you if they fight.
If there's anything I can do to help, let me know!

Your assessment of Austria's intentions is intriguing. If they truly intend to move against Russia and Germany, it could open opportunities for both of us in the Balkans and Aegean. I’ll keep an eye on their movements and watch closely for signs of a potential Austrian/Turkish alliance forming—or fracturing.

If a Turkish/Austrian alliance is indeed forming, I’m prepared to remain neutral or even obstruct it where it suits both of our interests. Let me know if there are specific directions you’d prefer me to watch closely—especially in the Adriatic or Balkans.

I'm probably not going to attack Russia in the first year. I think that will leave me to vulnerable to an attack from the north.
Are you considering opening north?
Italy seems a bit wary of you. I'm not sure how to read that

I appreciate your interest in southern stability—I’m monitoring movements along the Mediterranean closely. If you’re planning to push further south or west, I’d be happy to help secure the region for both of our benefit. Let me know where your focus lies.

Your interest in Tyrolia is noted—I’m currently focused on maintaining peace along my northern borders and may be able to support your ambitions there in exchange for a mutual understanding. Let’s keep the lines open.
I'm sure they're both lying to me then lol

Your suggestion about Greece as a mutual interest is compelling. While I’m not yet moving into the Aegean, I’ll be watching Austria’s movements closely—if they make a push eastward, I’d be open to aligning with you in the region.
Sure thing. I'll let you know what I hear.
I will if you do.

I'm inclined to agree—both Austria and Turkey seem to be testing the waters with each other, but their true intentions remain unclear. If they attempt to form a meaningful alliance, I’d appreciate your support in the Balkans to ensure it doesn’t become a long-term threat to either of us. I’ll keep a close eye on their diplomatic exchanges and movements.
I was a little noncommittal, so I'm sure that's it.
Good luck this turn.

I appreciate your openness about your intentions in Tyrolia and the Adriatic. I’ll continue to respect our mutual understanding along the border. Should you consider a shift toward the Aegean or Greece, I’d be interested in hearing your broader strategy—particularly if it opens opportunities for me further south.
Sounds good

Your concerns about Austria’s intentions are noted. If Austria shifts eastward or toward Greece, I’ll be ready to support your ambitions in the Aegean. I’m also keeping an eye on Russia’s movements in the Balkans—if you’re interested in coordinated action, let me know.
Thanks you too
I appreciate your openness, too. I think I'll be going east, but I'll let you know if that changes.
I understand. Let's play things safe this turn and see where it goes
What are you thinking about the Balkans?

That’s good to hear about your eastward focus. I’ll keep our border quiet and continue watching the Adriatic closely. If your plans evolve toward Greece or the Balkans later, feel free to let me know—I’d be happy to explore how we might align.
I think I'll probably take Serbia, and one of the others. If you and I can take Bulgaria and Rumania we'll be in a very strong position.
Ok, thank you. I'll do.
Thanks, and good luck this turn.

Understood. I’ll keep monitoring the Balkans and the Aegean closely. If Austria or Turkey shows signs of forming a lasting alliance, I’ll act in ways that support your long-term interests in the region.
If Austria continues eastward, it could create opportunities for us in the Aegean and Greece. I’ll remain watchful and open to coordination if the moment arises.
I agree. How are you feeling about Italy?
What have you been hearing from Turkey/austria?

From Turkey and Austria, I’ve sensed a tentative interest in aligning against external threats, but nothing firm yet. Turkey seems open to future coordination if Austria moves eastward or into the Balkans, and Austria appears focused on Tyrolia and possibly the Adriatic. I’ll keep monitoring their exchanges closely and let you know if I detect any concrete shifts in their strategy.

I’ve noticed your fleet’s positioning along the Mediterranean coast and I’m interested in understanding where you plan to focus your expansion. If you’re considering securing southern supply centers, we might find ways to cooperate on shared frontiers—particularly if Austria or Germany shifts southward.

Your interest in Tyrolia is noted—I’m currently maintaining a neutral stance along my northern borders. Should your strategy evolve further into the Adriatic or southern regions, I’d be open to coordination or support from Venice, depending on mutual benefit.

Interesting. I think Austria is being honest about trying to form a central triple alliance, but if you wanted to see how they react to that, I think you could start to make a bold move.
If you had any interest in being aggressive in the balkans I could always help you.
I'll keep you updated.
I'm cautiously optimistic, we'll see this turn.

I appreciate your openness about French movements. If their forces attempt to pressure southern Europe or the Iberian coast, I’d certainly appreciate an update. I’m also monitoring the Mediterranean closely—I’d welcome any insights you may have on fleet movements or coastal threats.
I'm glad to hear that, and I hope that we can work together in the future.
I'd say that's probably fair.
How are you feeling about the west?
Hey, Russia has told me that he's moving to Gal, so we might want to bounce him. If you're not okay with that, I understand.
Hi France, I'm not sure what Italy is planning, but I wouldn't be shocked if he moves toward you.
I think I'll move to Galicia regardless. I'd rather Russia not think I'm going to move there.
Thank you.
Of course! I'll let you know if I hear anything.
Sounds good. I will be sure to keep you updated if I learn anything.
I'll be doing some opening moves that are fairly standard, so as not to be seen as a threat. I'd be happy to cooperate against Germany if you're interested.
Thanks. I'll let you know what I find out.
I'll be doing some fairly standard opening moves, so that I am not seen as a threat. I'd be happy to cooperate against Germany if you're interested.
Alright, sounds good.
Austria and I will be bouncing in Tyr and Boh, so there's no real way for you to get into Gal without your fleet moving into Rum.
I see that Russia bounced you in BS, but other than that, things seem pretty good.
Yes, Russia and me arranged the bounce so we should be good.
I'd like that :)
Are you still planning to move to Rumania?
I was surprised by Italy's opening moves as well, but I may be able to help you contain him in the Mediterranean.

Your interest in Greece and the Aegean is noted—I’m watching Austria’s movements closely and remain open to coordinated action if they begin to encroach further east. I’ll keep you informed if I detect any meaningful shifts in their strategy.
Nice aggressive opening. I'll let you know if I hear anything
Thanks for honoring the DMZ in EC. Nice opening against Germany by the way.
I am not a fan of your movement into Piedmont. This does not bode well for our relationship.
Nice opening! Looks like you and Austria have a good position to work together
I'm pretty shocked as well. If you're going to be fighting Italy, though, then you'll need the builds. I'm willing to support you into Venice next turn.
Yep. That sounds good.
Thank you. I'll be in touch once I learn more.
Nice opening! Looks like Italy and Austria are working together. How do you want to handle this?

I assure you, my movement into Tuscany was purely defensive in nature. I remain focused on securing Italy’s interests and would be open to a more formal discussion about Mediterranean strategy, should you be interested.
What do you suggest?
Congrats on getting into Bur! I'm going to convoy to BEL this turn.
Hey Russia. I'm not sure how I feel about the whole Austria/russia situation. I'd be okay with you having Sweden if you move to Gal and Ukr this turn.
I was planning on it, but I need to cover Vienna now. I'm going to support you to Rumania, though.
Your optimism is appreciated, but I must emphasize that Italy remains neutral for now. I will keep your overtures in mind and will be sure to reach out when the timing feels right.
You're in an interesting place there.
I have no interest in conflict at this time, but I do appreciate your peace gesture. Let’s keep the channels open—perhaps we can explore mutual benefits in the future.
Understood.

Indeed, and I find myself in a delicate position. If your focus shifts toward the Balkans or northward, I’d be happy to ensure my forces remain in place to deter any southern incursions—mutual stability benefits us both.
Thanks for not moving to STP. I'll be using my fleet in North Sea to take Norway. You are welcome to have Sweden, but I'd prefer it if you didn't move your army there.
For the moment, they think I'm working with them so I could try to get Austria to help me get Rumania, but I'm not sure if they would buy that. Maybe I should move to Serbia instead?
You can't take Bel, you'll just bounce with France. I'd advise trying to get into the MAO.
Thank you for your kind words—I aim to keep things stable for now, which benefits all. Should your interests shift toward securing southern borders, I imagine our paths will cross again soon.
No problem. Thanks for letting me know about Norway!
Okay, so I can support Bur to Bel this turn?

Indeed, I am. The Balkans remain a powder keg, and Italy’s neutrality is not indifference—I watch everything closely. If your focus turns eastward, I’d hate to see your hands tied by southern pressures. Stability is in both our interests.
Ok, thanks. I'll move there from Bulgaria.
Hey, what's the plan for this turn?
Italy, I have to be honest and say I'm going to cover Vienna this turn. I hope you're not offended.
I'm going to try to get my build, but I'm worried that Italy is going to attack me.
Yep. That sounds good to me.
Yeah, I think I'll try to take Rumania myself this turn, maybe you could try to get Serbia?
Sure thing!
I don't think there's any chance of me getting Rumania this turn. I think I'll just move to Galicia.
Done.
True, but if he doesn't bounce me he can't get a build from Spain.

Austria’s recent comments about shifting focus toward the Balkans are intriguing. I’ll keep an eye on their army at Vienna and their fleet in Albania. If they begin to test their strength there, I’ll let you know—I imagine you’d appreciate a clear path to the Adriatic.
I'm worried too.
Let me know if you'd like to collaborate on anything, I'm sure we'll have opportunities to work together sooner or later.
Awesome.
Ok, I'll keep you updated.
Thanks.

I appreciate your understanding. Tuscany is a natural defensive anchor, and I remain open to discussions about shared Mediterranean interests—especially if the English fleet begins to press too hard toward the Ionian.
Austria will try to move to Rumania this turn.
Okay, I think I convinced him not to, so you should be good.
Cool.
Sounds good to me. Let me know if you need help with anything.
I appreciate your openness—I’m sure opportunities will arise soon. For now, I’m watching the Balkans closely, and if Austria’s peace gesture turns out to be a feint, I’ll be sure to let you know.
I'm telling Russia that I'm covering Vienna. I expect him to go to Galicia again, but he'll be stuck in Warsaw if he does.
I appreciate your support. Should the English fleet begin to test the southern Mediterranean, I’ll be sure to keep you informed—coordinated action could be mutually beneficial.
I think I'm going to move to Ruhr instead of Bur.

Your ambitions in the Aegean are clear, and I appreciate your patience. Should Austria’s peace offer prove hollow and their focus shift eastward, I may find myself with a small window of opportunity in the Adriatic. Let’s keep the lines open—coordinated pressure could be mutually rewarding.
Awesome, that works.

Your overtures toward peace are appreciated, and I remain open to dialogue. However, should your military posture shift toward the Balkans or northward, I’d hate to see your focus split with southern concerns. Stability benefits us both—I’ll keep that in mind.
It seems that Italy and Austria are working together. Do you know anything about it?

Your southern border is under no immediate threat, but I remain attentive to the region’s shifting dynamics. Should Austria’s intentions grow clearer or Turkey’s ambitions intensify, I’ll be sure to inform you promptly—perhaps we’ll find common ground sooner than expected.
Yes, I agree. I'll keep you updated.
Understood, thanks for the heads up
Sounds good

Your support is noted—I appreciate your openness. Should the English fleet begin to test the southern Mediterranean or if Austrian movements shift toward the Alps, I’ll be sure to let you know. Coordinated action could be mutually beneficial.
I'm still not sure if I should go for Serbia or Greece, though. What are your thoughts?

Your patience is appreciated—I remain attentive to the Balkans and the Adriatic. Should Austria’s peace gesture prove hollow or if their forces shift toward Trieste, I’ll ensure you’re the first to know. Coordinated pressure could be rewarding.
Your optimism is noted—I remain cautious but attentive to the southern front. If Austrian or Turkish movements threaten your interests, I’ll keep you informed. Opportunities may arise soon.
I'm not sure. I'm not sure what their long-term goal is, but I'll be sure to share any information I learn.

I’ve noticed your fleet remains stationed in Albania. If you intend to maintain peace, I’d recommend keeping pressure off the Balkans—Italy’s forces remain prepared to deter any southern incursions. Stability benefits both of us.
Italy has informed me that his moves are defensive. It may be possible that he is cooperating with you, but I will let you know if I find out more.
I can't speak for England, but I will do what I can to protect your interests in the Mediterranean.
Thank you for that. I'll do the same for you
Would you have any interest in heading east if Turkey and I could put aside any disagreements and fight together?
Thanks. I think he's planning to attack me, but he won't get anywhere.
Ok, thank you.
Thank you. I'll keep you updated.
It's hard for you to take both I think
Hey, are you going for Austria?
Sure thing. I'll convoy to PIC then.

Your patience is appreciated, but I must caution that Austria’s peace gestures may be a diversion. I’ve observed their army at Vienna closely. Should they move eastward, I’ll ensure you’re informed immediately—coordinated action could yield rewards.

I remain attentive to movements along your southern border. If Turkey’s ambitions grow or Austria’s focus shifts eastward, I’ll be sure to keep you informed. Opportunities for alignment may arise soon.

Heading east is an intriguing idea, though I remain cautious about entanglements at this stage. If Turkey’s ambitions grow and your southern border becomes a concern, I imagine our interests will naturally align. Until then, I’ll keep the lines open.
I hope you're right.

You're welcome—I appreciate your trust. I’ll remain watchful in the Adriatic and Aegean corridors, especially if Austria’s peace offer proves to be a feint. If tensions rise further east, I’ll be sure to let you know.

My interest in Austria remains limited for now—I’m focused on securing southern Europe’s stability. However, if your movements shift toward Vienna, I’ll ensure Italy’s position remains firm enough not to require your attention.

I hope you're right as well. I’ll keep my eyes on Austria’s fleet near Albania and their army at Vienna—any meaningful shift toward the Balkans will be reported promptly. If tensions grow further east, I imagine our interests will converge.
Yes, I know.
Hey Russia, what do you want to do about Galicia?
I'm going to try for Rumania, are you going to go for Greece?
Yes, I hope so.
I will let you know if I learn anything more.
I'm going to need to support myself to cover Vienna, but I'll take it in the spring.
Thanks.
Thanks for being open to it. Let me know if there's anything I can do to help you this turn.
Sure

I appreciate your optimism—I remain attentive to Austria’s movements along the Adriatic. Should their forces shift eastward toward Trieste or the Balkans, I’ll ensure you’re the first to know. A coordinated approach could be very rewarding.
Good luck!

Your offer is generous—I’ll keep it in mind. For now, I’m watching Austria’s peace gestures closely and ensuring Italy remains stable. If southern tensions grow or Turkey becomes more aggressive, I’ll reach out promptly. Your support is noted.
Are you there? I'm supporting you, but I need to know if you're supporting me as well.

I hope you're right as well. I’ll keep my eyes on Austria’s fleet near Albania and their army at Vienna—any meaningful shift toward the Balkans will be reported promptly. If tensions grow further east, I imagine our interests will converge.
Sounds good. Good luck!
Awesome!
I think I'm going to play it safe and go for Rumania
Good luck to you too.

I hope so as well. I’ve noticed Austria’s fleet remains stationed near Albania, and their army at Vienna hasn’t moved—so far, no sign of aggression eastward. If their focus shifts toward Trieste or the Balkans, I’ll ensure you’re the first to know. A coordinated approach could be very rewarding.
Yes, I'm supporting you as well.
Yes, let's hope they keep their word.

Thank you for your offer—I appreciate your support. For now, I’m watching the Adriatic closely and ensuring Italy remains stable. If southern tensions grow or Turkey becomes more aggressive, I’ll reach out promptly. Your openness is noted.
Yeah, I agree.
Awesome.

messages.message: I haven’t seen any concrete evidence of an Austro-Turkish alliance, but both are positioning aggressively in the Balkans. Austria’s recent peace overtures seem hollow—they’re likely trying to buy time while they secure their eastern flank. Turkey remains focused on the Aegean and seems open to cooperation if there’s a mutual benefit. I’ll keep an eye on both and let you know if I sense a real threat.
Thanks for the intel. Hopefully they keep that up, but if not I'm very concerned.

I’ve noticed Austria’s moves toward the Balkans and their growing influence in the region. If you’re interested in securing your southern flank, I’d be open to discussing mutual interests in the Aegean and Trieste. I’m not looking to expand at your expense, but cooperation could be mutually beneficial.

I’ve heard whispers from Turkey about increased naval activity near the Aegean. I’m not sure of the details, but I thought you might want to be aware. I remain open to discussing peaceful resolutions between us, especially if it helps ease tensions across your borders.
Yes, they seem to be coming for me now. I would definitely like to work together against Austria.

Thanks, I appreciate the information. I'm not sure what they're planning, but it looks like they're working with Russia. I'd be happy to coordinate with you, especially if they build a fleet in Smyrna.

I appreciate your interest in cooperation—I’m open to mutual defense or coordinated pressure against Austria, particularly in the Aegean and Trieste regions. If they continue to move aggressively toward you, I’d be willing to consider support, provided it doesn’t compromise my own southern interests. Let me know how you’d like to proceed.

Your cooperation offer is noted—I’d be open to discussing peaceful resolutions, especially if it helps stabilize the region. But I must warn you, Turkey’s recent moves suggest they’re preparing to act. If you’re considering building a fleet in Smyrna, I’d recommend moving quickly.

I’ve noticed England and Germany maintaining a strong northern presence, which could leave the Mediterranean vulnerable. I’d be interested in discussing how we might coordinate our efforts to ensure our mutual interests remain unchallenged. A quiet understanding could prove invaluable in the coming season.
If we have the chance, we should attack them now, before they become stronger. What do you suggest?
Thanks for holding up your end of the bargain. I've got some concerns about Turkey at the moment.
I'm building an army in Constantinople.
Thanks for not bouncing me in Sweden.
Hey, what happened with Bel?
Yeah, that's what I'm expecting. You might be able to get in position to help support me in the spring.
How would you like to coordinate our efforts?

I’ve been following Austria’s recent diplomatic gestures and their growing interest in peaceful resolutions. While I remain neutral, I’d be interested in hearing your perspective on how this might affect the broader Central European balance.
No problem. If you're concerned about Turkey, I might be able to help.
No problem. I'd appreciate it if you moved War out, though. It's making me a little nervous.
Nice job getting 3 builds!

I’d like to coordinate our Mediterranean efforts by ensuring that no single power gains dominance in the region. Perhaps we could agree to support each other’s fleets against English or German incursions. If you’re open to it, I’d suggest we focus on securing the southern routes first.
Alright. I will build a fleet in Marseilles, but I will move it to Spain.
Well, I'm not sure. I haven't really heard from Austria, but if he didn't see Russia as much of a threat, then I'm a little worried.

Your suggestion is noted—I’m positioning myself to be ready to support if needed. However, I’d prefer to keep the door open for multiple possibilities. If you’re considering a move into the Balkans, I’d appreciate knowing your timing so I can plan accordingly.
Thanks! I'm a bit worried about my situation in the North, but it will be worth it in the long run.

Your build in Constantinople is a strong move. I’m watching Austria’s intentions closely, and if they push too hard into your territory, I’d be open to a quiet agreement to support you in Trieste or the Aegean. Let me know if you’re interested in exploring that.

That’s a good move—positioning your fleet in Spain keeps pressure on England’s southern flank. I’d suggest coordinating our Mediterranean strategy further in Spring. If England tries to push into the region, we could support each other’s movements quietly.
How so?
So, what are you thinking about Russia?

I haven’t seen Austria’s full plans yet, but I’m inclined to believe they’re more interested in securing their eastern borders than challenging Russia directly. If you’re concerned, perhaps we could coordinate a quiet understanding on the Central European front.
Sure thing.
Also, I'd probably advise you to build a fleet.
I can understand why you'd be nervous, but I don't think there's anything you can do about it at the moment.
You're not actually a very strong ally, it seems.

I’ve noticed Austria’s fleet activity in the Adriatic. If they’re positioning for a move into Trieste, I’d be willing to support you there discreetly, provided we agree on mutual benefit in the region.
Yep. That was my plan
I'm still not sure what I'll do yet. Russia is at least saying he's worried about Turkey, so that could make things easier.
I'm not saying that I'm a strong ally, but I did give you Bel, so I'd say I've done a little bit.
Yeah, you and England can beat Germany easily, especially with Russia helping.

Russia’s concern over Turkey is a useful distraction, but it also leaves your southern flank exposed. If you’re uncertain about your next move, consider whether you’d be open to a temporary understanding with me regarding Trieste. I’m not looking for war—I’m looking for stability, and stability means no one power dominating the Adriatic. Let me know how you’d like to proceed.
No, that's fair. I was just trying to be a little cautious, but you're right that there's not really anything I can do about it.
Sounds good.
And you moved into Burgundy, which is a French territory.

I understand your concerns about Austria’s southern strategy. From what I’ve observed, Austria is more focused on balancing Turkey and Russia than directly threatening Germany. That said, I’d be happy to keep an eye on their movements and pass along any intelligence that might help you assess the Central European situation. If you’d prefer, I could also discreetly suggest to Austria that stability in the region benefits all parties.
Yep. Germany isn't really a very strong ally, though.
I'm planning on taking STP and moving to MAO

I appreciate your agreement to coordinate Mediterranean efforts. If England or Germany attempts to strengthen their foothold in the region, I’ll make sure to align our fleets accordingly. Perhaps we could also explore a quiet understanding about supporting each other’s builds in Spring 1902. I’d be happy to discuss further.
I think that's a good plan. I'd like to remain neutral between Austria and Russia.
I can help by supporting you into Bulgaria or Rumania.
Yeah, I can vacate Trieste for now. I'd be open to extending the deal, say 2 years, after which we can revisit.
Yeah, but that's a French territory. That's like me saying it's not okay for you to take Bel because you took Munich.

That’s a reasonable proposal. Vacating Trieste temporarily could help ease tensions and allow me to consolidate there without bloodshed. A two-year truce sounds acceptable, provided we both recognize Trieste as a contested center afterward. I’ll note your offer and let you know my position in Spring 1902.
Sounds good.

I respect your desire to remain neutral, but I’d caution you against ignoring Austria’s southern strategy entirely. While I don’t anticipate direct aggression toward Germany, a strong Austria could shift the balance in Central Europe. I’ll keep monitoring developments and pass along any relevant intelligence discreetly.
What do you mean by supporting each other's builds?

By supporting each other's builds, I mean quietly coordinating our expansions in Spring 1902 to avoid mutual competition in the Mediterranean. For instance, if England or Germany tries to pressure us from the north, we could ensure our fleets complement each other's movements. We could also agree to protect each other’s supply centers in the region if needed.
Alright, sounds good.
I'm assuming you mean Bulgaria, and that would be great if you could help me with that.
I can also help you take Sweden if you'd like

I’ve noticed Austria’s fleet in the Adriatic is becoming more active. If they attempt a move into Trieste, I’d be willing to offer quiet support to you there, provided we agree on a shared vision for the Aegean. No need to alarm others—we’ll handle it discreetly.
Understood.
That makes no sense, considering it was you who took Belgium in the first place.

Your proposal for a temporary truce regarding Trieste is intriguing. I remain open to stability in the region and would be willing to consider a two-year truce if it benefits both of us. I’ll keep your offer in mind and respond formally in Spring.
That sounds good to me.
Yeah, so it's fair for you to get Bel.

That’s encouraging—I appreciate your openness to coordination. To clarify, I suggest we avoid any direct declarations to keep things quiet, but agree on mutual support against English or German moves into the Mediterranean. In Spring 1902, I’ll be watching how England and Germany position themselves, and we can adjust accordingly. Perhaps we could also consider coordinating our builds to ensure neither of us outcompetes the other.

Regarding the fleet question, I see no reason not to build one; the Mediterranean is a contested space, and a quiet but assertive naval presence could serve both our interests in the long run. Let’s revisit the Trieste matter in Spring with fresh eyes.

For now, I’ll keep Trieste under observation, and if the moment is right, we’ll ensure no single power dominates the region.

Sounds good

In Spring, I’ll be watching Trieste closely; if Austria is still hesitant, I’d be open to a quiet discussion about how we might ensure no one power dominates the Adriatic.
